items.title: BN aims to wrest Johor Jaya from DAP after 13 years
items.summary: PASIR GUDANG: Barisan Nasional (BN) is aiming to recapture Johor Jaya from DAP in the coming state election, an urban seat it last won 13 years ago. Johor BN Youth chief Noor Azleen Ambros said the coalition was also determined to retain Permas. Noor Azleen, who is also Pasir Gudang Umno Youth chief, said these were among the key issues discussed at the Pasir Gudang BN parliamentary meeting last night.

items.title: Motorcyclist killed in Jalan Reko crash
items.summary: District police chief Assistant Commissioner Naazron Abdul Yusof said the incident occurred about 11.13pm last night. "The motorcyclist, a local technician, was travelling from Putrajaya towards his home in Semenyih when the incident happened. Naazron said the motorcyclist was confirmed dead at the scene due to the severity of the impact.

items.title: Housing Ministry revives over 1,500 sick and abandoned projects worth RM148b, says Nga Kor Ming
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 6 — More than 1,500 sick and abandoned private housing projects nationwide have been successfully revived by the Ministry of Housing and Local Government’s (KPKT) special task force from 2023 to May this year. Housing and Local Government Minister Nga Kor Ming said the recovery efforts involved 1,576 projects comprising 188,525 housing units, with a total estimated gross development value (GDV) of RM148.21 billion. He said the achievement reflected KPKT’s continued commitment to strengthening the nation’s housing sector through the Task Force on Sick and Abandoned Private Housing Projects (TFST), which was established in 2023.

items.title: Zahid proposes dedicated accreditation body for TVET institutions
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi has proposed the establishment of a dedicated accreditation body for Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) institutions, separate from existing academic and skills accreditation agencies. Zahid, who is also National TVET Council chairman, said the move was necessary given the presence of 1,395 TVET institutions nationwide. He said several developed countries already operated separate accreditation bodies for technical education, a model Malaysia could emulate.

items.title: No sign of missing climber Jaslinda as Gunung Batu Putih search enters 14th day
items.summary: IPOH, June 6 — The search and rescue (SAR) operation for Jaslinda Saludin, 49, who went missing in the Gunung Batu Putih area, has entered its 14th day without any new leads, although the search has continued on a smaller scale since Thursday. He said that as of Thursday, eight members of the Special, Tactical and Rescue Team of Malaysia (STORM) and two Forest Mountain Guides (MGP) were still involved in the search sector location, and all of them safely exited at 11.30 am. “Yesterday, there were five search teams consisting of members from the Forestry Department and MGP who entered the search sector location.

items.title: Agong orders swift gazetting of KL green spaces, flood retention ponds, says Hannah Yeoh
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 6 — His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, has decreed that there should be no delay in gazetting Kuala Lumpur’s green spaces and flood retention ponds, Federal Territories Minister Hannah Yeoh said today. “The Yang di-Pertuan Agong decreed that we must not delay the decision on the gazetting of areas such as green zones and flood retention ponds, because these are areas of public interest. Separately, Yeoh said Kuala Lumpur already has sufficient infrastructure and that the current focus is on improving and revitalising public recreational spaces, particularly for families.

items.title: Senior cop misconduct cases on IPCC radar, warns deputy IGP
items.summary: KUCHING: Several integrity-related cases involving senior police officers ranked Senior Assistant Commissioner and above are now on the radar of the Independent Police Conduct Commission (IPCC). Deputy Inspector-General of Police Tan Sri Ayob Khan Mydin Pitchay said he had received public complaints and that such cases be lodged with the IPCC. He said police officers and personnel must no longer assume they are only being monitored by the Integrity and Standard Compliance Department, as the Home Ministry also has a dedicated agency overseeing them.

items.title: Rafizi: Bersama to contest Johor and Negri Sembilan state elections
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Parti Bersama Malaysia leader Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli has confirmed that the party will contest in the upcoming Johor and Negri Sembilan state elections. Speaking to reporters after setting up a public complaints and party membership registration counter at the Pandan Indah market here this morning, Rafizi said the party will begin accepting applications from prospective candidates next Monday (June 8) as part of its selection process, Sin Chew Daily reported. When asked whether the three-week-old party would have sufficient time to prepare candidates for the elections, Rafizi said applications would open on Monday and all applicants would undergo interviews and assessments.

items.title: Smuggling bid sinks in RM57.15mil drug bust in Muar
items.summary: MUAR: Police have busted a syndicate believed to be smuggling drugs to a neighbouring country by sea, seizing narcotics worth RM57.15 million in a raid at Parit Amat Laut yesterday. Region Two Marine Police commander Assistant Commissioner Noor Azman Jamal said the operation was launched following intelligence on smuggling activities in the area. He said the raiding team spotted a boat and several vehicles at a coastal area off Muar shortly after midnight.

items.title: 1,576 sick, abandoned housing projects worth RM148bil revived since 2023
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The government has revived more than 1,576 sick and abandoned housing projects nationwide since 2023, with an estimated gross development value (GDV) of RM148.21 billion. Housing and Local Government Minister Nga Kor Ming said the recovered projects involved 188,525 housing units. Nga said this reflected the government's commitment to addressing longstanding issues involving stalled housing developments and protecting homebuyers affected by project delays.

items.title: Nga Kor Ming says 4,000 litterbugs now paying fines while sweeping Kuala Lumpur streets for free
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 6 — As of today, over 4,000 litterbugs have been caught so far, said Housing and Local Government (KPKT) minister Nga Kor Ming. Nga said that the Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) now has 4,000 free social workers to help clean up the streets of KL. “KL is going to have not 1,000 but 10,000 facial recognition and high definition CCTV.

items.title: PM calls for specialised programmes to address talent pool readiness
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Major investors, including Intel, Infineon and Amazon Web Services (AWS), have raised concerns about the readiness of Malaysia's talent pool, says Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. Anwar said this underscored the importance of stronger collaboration between the government and the private sector. Anwar called for greater private sector participation in training programmes.

items.title: Pahang ruler briefed on federal allocations for the state, says Amir Hamzah
items.summary: PETALING JAYA: Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan has briefed the Pahang ruler, Al-Sultan Abdullah Ri'ayatuddin Al-Mustafa Billah Shah, on Putrajaya's allocations to the state. Amir Hamzah said that during an audience with Al-Sultan Abdullah last week, he explained that federal government allocations and development projects for Pahang had increased year-on-year. Amir Hamzah said the government remained committed to developing Pahang through various initiatives and direct assistance for the people.

items.title: Mara TVET institutions lead with 99.5pct employability rate
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Majlis Amanah Rakyat's (Mara) network of technical and vocational education and training (TVET) institutions lead the way with an employability rate of 99.5 per cent, said Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi. Zahid congratulated Mara on the performance of all 270 of its TVET institutions, which he said were at the forefront of the national TVET network. "They have achieved an employability rate of approximately 99.5 per cent for Mara TVET graduates," he said at the launch of TVET 2.0 at National TVET Day 2026.

items.title: Myanmar teen caught with fake UNHCR card in Immigration raid
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: An alleged RM200 fake refugee card proved futile for a teenager who was detained during an Immigration operation at a squatter settlement along Jalan Klang Lama here early this morning. The 19-year-old Myanmar teen was among those screened in Op Sapu where officers later discovered a suspected counterfeit United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees (UNHCR) card in his possession during the checks. When questioned, the teenager said he had paid a friend to obtain the card about a year ago.

items.title: Amir Hamzah assures Sultan Abdullah of consistent federal aid and ECRL boost for Pahang
items.summary: PETALING JAYA, June 6 — Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan met with the Sultan of Pahang, Al-Sultan Abdullah Ri’ayatuddin Al-Mustafa Billah Shah, last week to provide clarification on federal allocations and assistance channelled to the state. He said the audience enabled him to give a comprehensive briefing covering federal allocations to Pahang, which have shown yearly increases, as well as focusing on priority projects proposed by the state government. “From the briefing, His Royal Highness was able to see that there is indeed a commitment from the federal government to assist states, including Pahang, through the implementation of development projects, as well as allocations for the state, which have been increasing consistently year by year.

items.title: Mydin: Essential goods supply remains stable despite global uncertainties
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The supply of essential goods in Malaysia remains stable despite ongoing uncertainties in global supply chains, says Mydin Mohamed Holdings Bhd (Mydin) managing director Datuk Dr Ameer Ali Mydin. On imported goods, Ameer Ali said local retailers have adopted forward-buying strategies to ensure a consistent and adequate supply of essential imported products. "Supplies remain stable and adequate.

items.title: Police hunt Indonesian suspects over robbery, housebreaking cases in Manjung
items.summary: IPOH: Police are looking for a group of Indonesians believed to be behind eight robbery and housebreaking cases around Ayer Tawar and Pekan Gurney in Manjung this year. Manjung police chief Assistant Commissioner Hasbullah Abd Rahman said the group, comprising three to four men, targeted homes located on the fringes of forests and in remote areas. He said those with information on this group should contact the Manjung district police headquarters operations room at 05-688 6222, the WhatsApp hotline at 017-682 8005, or any nearby police station to assist in investigations.

items.title: Nga: KL ranked 65th in smart city index as skyscrapers soar and tourists flood in
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 6 — Housing and Local Government Ministry (KPKT) minister Nga Kor Ming revealed that Kuala Lumpur now ranks 65th in the global smart city index. Speaking at the launch of the KL Architecture Festival’s (KLAF) Tropical Fruit Pavilion launch at Titiwangsa Lake Garden this morning, Nga also said that KL is among the top 10 most popular cities in the world in terms of international tourist arrivals according to Mastercard. The launch was also attended by the Federal Territories minister Hannah Yeoh as well as KL mayor Datuk Fadlun Mak Ujud.

items.title: [UPDATED] Talk things out, don't use courts,' says Zahid on Negri Sembilan crisis
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi has called for the political crisis in Negri Sembilan involving the four Undang and its ruler Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir, to be resolved through negotiation rather than be brought before a tribunal or court. The Negri Sembilan adat and constitutional crisis has drawn attention after the Undang Yang Empat reportedly exercised powers to remove Tuanku Muhriz as the state ruler. The Negri Sembilan assembly was dissolved yesterday.

items.title: Strict SOPs block 50 volunteers from joining search for Jaslinda
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Standard operating procedure (SOP) restrictions have prevented roughly 50 volunteers from joining the search and rescue (SAR) operation for missing hiker Jaslinda Saludin. Kelantan Forestry Mountain Guide (MGP) Roshamrizal Zahari urged the Perak Forestry Department and the state Fire and Rescue Department to ease these rules, saying that many eager volunteers include former commandos and active-duty military personnel. Perak Fire and Rescue Department assistant director Sabarozi Nor Ahmad said that volunteers must apply and undergo screening for safety and coordination.

items.title: Work today, gone tomorrow: Bukit Aman warns of maid scam
items.summary: Bukit Aman Commercial Crime Investigation Department director Datuk Rusdi Mohd Isa said the syndicate lures victims by offering fast maid placement services, attractive deposit rates and seemingly legitimate documents. He said the syndicate often requests additional payments under various pretexts after securing the victim's trust. Always verify, stay alert and report immediately if you become a victim," he said.

items.title: MACC launches AI-powered MACCAMPUS platform to boost anti-corruption education in universities
items.summary: KUALA NERUS, June 6 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) today launched MACCAMPUS, a digital platform incorporating artificial intelligence (AI) and gamification to strengthen integrity education and anti-corruption awareness among tertiary students. MACC Chief Commissioner Datuk Seri Abd Halim Aman said the initiative reflects their commitment to engaging university students in enhancing integrity education, corruption prevention and positive values through smartphones and information technology applications. “To ensure MACC remains capable of engaging university students consistently and effectively, it requires a more innovative, interactive and technology-driven educational approach,” he said when speaking at the Integrity Financial Literacy @ Student Anti-Corruption Programme 2026 at Dewan Sultan Mizan, UMT, here.

items.title: [UPDATED] PM: RM50mil allocated for TVET 2.0 but companies must do their part
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The government has allocated RM50 million from the training programme fund under the Skills Development Fund Corporation (PTPPK) for the TVET 2.0 initiative launched here today, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said. "Let me restate: the government is approving up to RM50 million from the high-impact programme fund under PTPK to be used for TVET 2.0. "There is, however, a condition — employers and industry must cooperate with the government, particularly within the National TVET Council, to make it a success, so that there is proper coordination," he said at the launch of TVET 2.0 at National TVET Day 2026.

items.title: Shortage of 49 doctors behind long waiting times at Segamat Hospital
items.summary: SEGAMAT: The Health Ministry has been urged to immediately deploy more personnel to Segamat Hospital to address a shortage of 49 doctors that has resulted in long waiting times for patients. Johor Health and Environment Committee chairman Ling Tian Soon said the hospital needs 169 medical personnel to manage its various departments, several of which operate 24 hours a day. Ling said he visited the hospital following complaints over long waiting times.

items.title: Malaysia diversifying energy supply sources to ensure security, says Amir Hamzah
items.summary: PETALING JAYA, June 6 — The government is using a sound risk management approach by diversifying energy supply sources to ensure the country’s energy supply is sufficient, said Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan. He said the government, through Petroliam Nasional Bhd and oil companies, remains committed to seeking new supply sources, including from South Africa and the United States, to ensure the continuity of economic activities is not affected. “For coal, Malaysia continues to depend largely on Indonesia while expanding its supply sources to other countries, including the United States and Australia.

items.title: BN to contest all 36 Negri Sembilan seats
items.summary: JELEBU: Barisan Nasional (BN) will contest all 36 state seats in the Negri Sembilan state election, says Umno state chairman Datuk Seri Jalaluddin Alias. He added that a list of candidates for the state election will be submitted to party president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi for review. Also present were Undang Luak Jelebu Datuk Maarof Mat Rashad; Klawang assemblyman Datuk Bakri Sawir; Sungai Lui assemblyman Datuk Mohd Razi Mohd Ali; and Jelebu Islamic affairs officer and registrar of marriages, divorces and reconciliations Abdul Manas Md Isa.

items.title: Iran strikes US bases in Kuwait, Bahrain after drone attacks on Iran territory
items.summary: HAMILTON, Canada: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) said it struck "enemy bases" in Kuwait and Bahrain after United States (US) drones attacked sites on Iranian territory, Anadolu Ajansi (AA) reported, citing Tasnim News on Saturday. The IRGC's Aerospace Force targeted the Ali al-Salem air base in Kuwait and key US Navy Fifth Fleet facilities in Bahrain with ballistic missiles, according to the semi-official news agency. Iran, in retaliation, subsequently launched strikes against Israel and targeted countries hosting US military bases, while also disrupting shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, a critical route for global energy supplies.

items.title: Fahmi Fadzil pays tribute to late Pahang PKR leader Abas Awang
items.summary: KUANTAN, June 6 — Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil has conveyed his condolences to the family of Pahang PKR leader Abas Awang, who died yesterday. In a Facebook post today, Fahmi said Abas’s contributions, sacrifices and service to the party and the people will always be remembered. Abas died at his residence in Section 4, Bandar Baru Bangi, Selangor.

items.title: Secret gambling den uncovered in squatter raid; 78 foreigners detained
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: An Immigration Department operation in Jalan Klang Lama early this morning uncovered an illegal online gambling den operating within a squatter settlement following checks on about 40 homes. The gambling outlet was discovered during Op Sapu, which led to the detention of 78 undocumented migrants. A man found inside the gambling outlet attempted to mislead authorities when questioned.

items.title: Four suspected cable thieves caught on video along MRR2, police launch manhunt
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 6 — Police are tracking four men suspected of stealing cables along the side of the Middle Ring Road 2 (MRR2) here after a video of the incident went viral on social media. Ampang Jaya District Police Chief ACP Khairul Anuar Khalid said police detected a viral video at about 5 pm yesterday showing several individuals believed to be stealing cables from a manhole along the highway. Khairul Anuar said the complainant managed to record the incident before lodging the report and the case is being investigated under Section 431A of the Penal Code and Section 235 of the Communications and Multimedia Act 1998.

items.title: Onn Hafiz likely to defend Machap seat in upcoming Johor polls
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Caretaker Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi is expected to defend his Machap state seat in the upcoming Johor elections . However, Jais' focus is to support any BN candidate, particularly those contesting seats within the Kluang area. "Regardless of who the candidate is, the Kluang Umno and BN machinery will support our representative," said Jais, who is also the Kluang Umno division chief.

items.title: MARA TVET institutions record 99.5pc graduate employability rate, says Zahid
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 6 (Bernama) -- A total of 270 Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) institutions under Majlis Amanah Rakyat (MARA) have recorded a graduate employability rate of 99.5 per cent, Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said. He said the impressive achievement demonstrated that MARA’s TVET institutions are at the forefront of advancing the nation’s TVET agenda, which has now entered the TVET 2.0 phase. “They have embraced TVET 2.0 not only through the various contributions I mentioned earlier, but also by signing agreements with the National TVET Council and most TVET institutions across the country to ensure that all TVET students will be employed by the industry, with 100 per cent absorption into the workforce,” said Ahmad Zahid, who is also chairman of the National TVET Council, in his welcoming remarks at the National TVET Day 2026 celebration here today.

items.title: [UPDATED] Johor polls: Zahid to meet MCA, MIC tomorrow to finalise BN candidates
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Barisan Nasional (BN) chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi will hold discussions with the coalition's component parties tomorrow to finalise their candidates for the Johor election, with MCA and MIC still working out their positions. Zahid, who is also Umno president, confirmed that BN would contest all 56 seats in the Johor election. On Thursday, Zahid said BN will only unveil its candidates for the Johor election a week before nomination day.

items.title: 50 years on: Double Six tragedy a reminder to defend Sabah's rights, says Donald Mojuntin
items.summary: By Olivia Miwil Pictures by Mohd Adam Arinin KOTA KINABALU: The 50th anniversary of the Double Six tragedy should serve as a reminder for Sabahans to remain united in defending the state's rights, dignity and future, said Moyog assemblyman Datuk Donald Peter Mojuntin. Donald, 61, is the son of late Datuk Peter Mojuntin who was the local government and housing minister. Other victims were the then chief minister Tun Fuad Stephens, Datuk Salleh Sulong (Sabah finance minister), Chong Thien Vun (Sabah works and communication minister), Datuk Wahid Peter Andu (secretary to Sabah finance minister), Dr Syed Hussin Wafa (state economic planning director), and Datuk Ishak Atan (private secretary to then Federal Minister Tengku Razaleigh Hamzah.

items.title: [UPDATED] PM orders all govt training bodies to open slots for TVET students
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: All government training and educational bodies will be required to provide training opportunities to TVET students nationwide, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said today. "I am asking the Chief Secretary (Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar) to ensure that all government training and educational institutions are required to provide initial training opportunities to 50 to 100 participants at each TVET institution," he said in an officiating speech at the Hari TVET Negara, Dataran Putrajaya today. He said the participants will be placed into various training centres including crypto centres or other training bodies affiliated to the National Science Council.

items.title: Motorcyclist killed in crash after sudden exit from Cheras junction
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A 32-year-old motorcyclist was killed in a collision with a car at a T-junction along Jalan Permaisuri in Bandar Mahkota Cheras last night. Kajang police chief Assistant Commissioner Naazron Abdul Yusof said initial investigations indicated that the car, driven by a 35-year-old trader, was travelling along the road before approaching the junction. Naazron said the motorcyclist, who worked as a bus driver, was pronounced dead at the scene.

items.title: Police hunt men seen stealing cables in broad daylight along MRR2 [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police are tracking down four men seen stealing utility cables along the Middle Ring Road 2 (MRR2) in a video that has gone viral. Ampang Jaya police chief Assistant Commissioner Khairul Anuar Khalid said a witness saw the men loading cables into a grey Honda Odyssey on Thursday and lodged a police report. He said checks revealed that the Honda Odyssey's registration number belonged to another vehicle.

items.title: Teacher in Sabah interior shapes students into community change-makers
items.summary: KENINGAU: The common perception that vocational education merely trains students in technical skills has proven inaccurate in this interior district of Sabah. Through a community-based learning approach he introduced, Sirhajwan guides students not only to become skilled workers but also agents of change who actively contribute to society. "I developed a holistic learning formula based on four key approaches: direct service, indirect service, advocacy and community-based research," he told Bernama.

items.title: Double Six tragedy, 50 years on
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: From simple gatherings and prayers to elaborate ceremonial parades, the "Double Six" commemorative event has evolved the last 50 years, touching the hearts of many. Sembulan resident Affendi Surat 57, said he saw how things have evolved around the historical site the last 50 years. Borneo History Society founder and president Dr Shari Jeffri said the Double Six tragedy was a devastating event for Sabah.

items.title: Anwar: Selected TVET students to receive AI and cryptology training under new govt initiative
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 6 — All government training and research institutions will be required to provide training programmes in emerging fields for selected Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) students beginning July, said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. He said at least 50 to 100 TVET students at each selected institution will be given early training opportunities in emerging and high-technology fields such as artificial intelligence (AI) and cryptology. Also present were Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi, ministers and Shamsul Azri.

items.title: Anwar: Govt approves up to RM50m from PTPK fund to boost TVET 2.0 programme
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 6 — The government has agreed to allow the use of up to RM50 million from the High-Impact Programme fund under the Skills Development Fund Corporation (PTPK) to strengthen the implementation of Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) 2.0, said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. Anwar, who is also Finance Minister, said the use of the fund is subject to conditions, namely that employers and industry players must work closely with the government to ensure the success of the TVET 2.0 agenda. This will also enhance training programmes and exposure opportunities,” he said when officiating the National TVET Day 2026 celebration here today.

items.title: Two-year petrol smuggling activity to Thailand uncovered in Tumpat raid, six detained
items.summary: TUMPAT, June 6 — Six individuals have been arrested following the discovery of an illegal petrol storage facility allegedly used to stockpile fuel for smuggling into Thailand in an operation that has been going on for about two years. Brigade Southeast PGA commander Senior Assistant Commissioner Ahmad Radzi Hussain said the operation was launched following intelligence on suspected fuel smuggling activities at a premises in Tualang, Pengkalan Kubor. He said surveillance conducted for about 30 minutes showed several four-wheel-drive vehicles, believed to be Mitsubishi Pajero units, entering the premises in a suspicious manner before enforcement teams moved in.

items.title: Cops hunt dark-robed thief for stealing laundry
items.summary: AMPANG JAYA: Police are tracking down a suspect caught on camera stealing laundry from a clothesline at a terraced house in Taman Cheras Indah. A video of the suspect, who was clad in a dark robe, went viral on social media. Ampang Jaya police chief Assistant Commissioner Khairul Anuar Khalid said police were alerted to the clip at 5pm yesterday.

items.title: Mydin says essential goods supply in Malaysia remains stable despite global uncertainty
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 6 — The supply of essential goods in Malaysia remains stable despite ongoing uncertainties in global supply chains, says Mydin Mohamed Holdings Bhd (Mydin) managing director Datuk Dr Ameer Ali Mydin. Planting and harvesting activities are proceeding as usual, while fertiliser supplies remain sufficient. On imported goods, Ameer Ali said local retailers have adopted forward-buying strategies to ensure a consistent and adequate supply of essential imported products.

items.title: FamilyMart apologises, says delivery rider’s wellbeing attended to after viral soup incident
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 6 — FamilyMart Malaysia has apologised over an incident involving a delivery rider who was allegedly splashed with hot soup in a viral video, saying it has already attended to the rider’s wellbeing. FamilyMart Malaysia said the company has taken steps to ensure the delivery rider’s welfare is being addressed, while also cooperating fully with the authorities in their ongoing investigation. The incident surfaced after a 23-second video began circulating online on May 28 showing a heated exchange between a delivery rider and a FamilyMart employee.

items.title: Terrifying LPT2 crash leaves woman, 57, with severed leg, four others injured
items.summary: DUNGUN, June 6 — A woman lost her left leg after the car she was travelling in was involved in an accident at Kilometre 341.5 of the East Coast Expressway 2 (LPT2), near the Paka Rest and Service (R&R) area near here, yesterday. Operations Commander senior fire officer II Nor Hazamullah Abdullah said the 57-year-old woman suffered the injury when the Proton X70 sports utility vehicle (SUV) she was travelling in skidded off the road at the scene. “The victim, whose left leg was severed below the knee, was successfully extricated by firefighters.

items.title: Police tracking road bully after alleged helmet attack on car windscreen in Ipoh
items.summary: IPOH, June 6 — Police are tracking down a motorcyclist believed to have smashed a car windscreen with a helmet in an incident along Jalan Raja Ashman Shah here early yesterday morning. Ipoh district police chief ACP Muhammad Najib Hamzah said police received a report from the victim, a local man, regarding the incident, which occurred at about 1.26 am. “Initial investigations found that the incident occurred after the victim honked at a motorcyclist who was riding erratically and nearly got in his way.

items.title: Four-year-old dies after drowning in swimming pool at Port Dickson resort
items.summary: PORT DICKSON, June 6 — A four-year-old boy died after drowning in a swimming pool at a resort along Jalan Pantai, Batu 4, here yesterday. Port Dickson deputy police chief DSP Mohd Ruslan Mat Kib said police received a report from Port Dickson Hospital at 7.26 pm regarding the incident. Mohd Ruslan said the boy was pronounced dead at 10.31 pm and a post-mortem confirmed drowning as the cause of death.

items.title: Dzulkefly: No healthcare facility, including Segamat Hospital, will be shut down
items.summary: MELAKA, June 6 — The government has assured that no healthcare facilities in the country, including Segamat Hospital in Johor, will be shut down, Health Minister Datuk Seri Dzulkefly Ahmad said. He said the situation at Segamat Hospital stemmed from service pressures and did not involve any suspension of services, adding that the Health Ministry had activated a cluster crisis management mechanism to ensure patient care remains uninterrupted. Also present were Health Ministry secretary-general Datuk Seri Hasnol Zam Zam Ahmad and Melaka Health Department director Dr Husnina Ibrahim.

items.title: Melaka CM hints state election may be held after Johor, Negri Sembilan polls
items.summary: ALOR GAJAH, June 6 — Melaka Chief Minister Datuk Seri Ab Rauf Yusoh has hinted that the state election will be held after the Johor and Negeri Sembilan polls are concluded. The current term of the Melaka State Legislative Assembly ends on Dec 30. Ab Rauf further said he would examine the results of the Johor and Negeri Sembilan state elections before deciding on a suitable date to dissolve the state assembly.

items.title: AG seeks Federal Court leave to appeal ruling allowing Bar’s challenge to Zahid’s DNAA
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 6 — The Attorney-General (AG) is seeking leave to appeal against the decision of the Court of Appeal that had allowed the Malaysian Bar to challenge the prosecution’s decision to halt all further proceedings against Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi in the Yayasan Akalbudi corruption case. Ahmad Zahid, who is also the second proposed respondent in the judicial review proceedings, filed a separate application for leave to appeal on May 25. The court granted leave for the Bar to commence judicial review against the Attorney-General’s impugned decision, thereby setting aside the Kuala Lumpur High Court’s decision dated June 27, 2024.

items.title: Anwar to launch National TVET Day 2026 celebration in Putrajaya today
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 6 (Bernama) -- Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim will officiate the National Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) Day 2026 celebration at Dataran Putrajaya today, underscoring the government’s continued commitment to developing a highly skilled workforce. Anwar is scheduled to deliver an address at 10 am and officially launch the three-day event, which began yesterday under the theme TVET Pilihan Utama Kerjaya (TVET, The Preferred Career Path). The celebration is expected to attract 500,000 visitors and will highlight TVET’s role in developing a highly skilled workforce and strengthening the nation’s economic competitiveness.

items.title: DKU secretary seeks contempt action against six over alleged defiance of court order in Negri Sembilan royal dispute
items.summary: SEREMBAN, June 6 — Dewan Keadilan dan Undang (DKU) secretary Raja Norazli Raja Nordin plans to initiate contempt of court proceedings against six individuals, including three Undangs, for allegedly defying a High Court order linked to an ongoing dispute involving the royal institution, The Star reported. Raja Norazli said the move followed reports that some of the plaintiffs had proceeded with actions related to the proclamation of a Yang di-Pertuan Besar despite an ad interim injunction granted by the High Court yesterday. According to The Star, Raja Norazli said the court order restrained the plaintiffs from convening or holding any DKU meeting, including a Special Sitting scheduled for yesterday.

items.title: Malaysia Embassy confirms no citizens affected in Lufthansa incident at Frankfurt Airport
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 5 — No Malaysians were affected after a Lufthansa Boeing 787-9 aircraft suffered a nose landing gear failure while preparing for departure at Frankfurt Airport on June 4, the Embassy of Malaysia in Germany confirmed. In a statement posted on Facebook, the embassy said that, according to information provided by the relevant German authorities and Lufthansa, the aircraft experienced a nose landing gear failure while parked at a gate in preparation for its scheduled departure to Los Angeles. German Press Agency (dpa) reported that several employees were injured when a Lufthansa aircraft collapsed while parked at the gate at Frankfurt Airport, for reasons yet unknown.

items.title: MACC officer tells Lim Guan Eng trial he was unaware of Zarul Ahmad’s alleged links to rival tunnel bidder
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 5 — An investigating officer told the Sessions Court today that he was unaware that former Consortium Zenith Construction Sdn Bhd (CZCSB) director Datuk Zarul Ahmad Mohd Zulkifli had links to another construction company that participated in the open tender for the Penang undersea tunnel and three main roads project. Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) Headquarters Investigation Division Senior Superintendent Zulhilmi Ramli said that besides Consortium Zenith Beijing Urban Construction Group (CZBUCG), VST Cemerlang Sdn Bhd had also submitted a bid for the mega project through the open tender process. However, he said he was unsure whether Zarul Ahmad had planted VST Cemerlang as a bidder, and only knew that the businessman had links with CZBUCG.

items.title: Gobind says MyGOV app to carry RTM’s Fifa World Cup 2026 broadcasts
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 5 — Malaysians will be able to watch the Fifa World Cup 2026 through the government’s MyGOV Malaysia app. Digital Minister Gobind Singh Deo said the platform was part of the government’s broader effort to improve access to digital government services. “The MyGOV Malaysia app reflects the Madani Government’s continued efforts to improve how Malaysians access digital services.

items.title: DPM Zahid: Govt targets 650,000 TVET enrolments in 2026
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 5 — The government is targeting at least 650,000 students to enrol in Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) programmes at 1,395 TVET institutions nationwide by December 31 this year. Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said the target follows a significant rise in student participation in TVET, which is increasingly becoming a preferred education and career pathway among parents and students. “Last year, about 587,000 students enrolled in TVET institutions, compared with around 121,000 when the TVET Council was first established.

items.title: No discussions on reviving Muafakat Nasional 2.0, says Zahid
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 5 — Umno president Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi today dismissed suggestions of reviving Muafakat Nasional (MN) 2.0, saying there have been no discussions on the matter between Umno and PAS. Ahmad Zahid, who is also deputy prime minister, said Umno and Barisan Nasional’s (BN) priority remains ensuring political stability through the unity government formed with Pakatan Harapan (PH) and parties from Sabah and Sarawak. Yesterday, PAS Youth proposed that Umno revive political cooperation through Muafakat Nasional ahead of the upcoming Johor state election.

items.title: Pahang Sultan graces launch of gym for firefighters at Indera Mahkota Station
items.summary: KUANTAN, June 5 — The Sultan of Pahang Al-Sultan Abdullah Ri’ayatuddin Al-Mustafa Billah Shah today officiated the opening of the Mahkota Gymnasium at the Indera Mahkota Fire and Rescue Station, a reflection of His Royal Highness’ concern for the fitness and well-being of rescue personnel. The Pahang Ruler arrived at about 3pm and was welcomed by Malaysian Fire and Rescue Department director-general Nor Hisham Mohammad, Pahang Fire and Rescue Department director Datuk Mohd Razam Taja Rahim and Universal Fitness & Leisure Sdn Bhd (UFL) managing director Datuk Radha Krishnan. The Sultan later received a briefing from Mohd Razam on the development and operation of the gymnasium, which is equipped with exercise equipment donated by His Royal Highness.
